answer:
**Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*** engages extensively with progress (moral, industrial, societal), improvement, perfectibility (of races and faculties), and perfecting (of arts, forces, etc.).[^1][^2][^3]

**Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*** similarly discusses improvement and perfecting across industries (agricultural, manufacturing, commerce), agents of production, and human faculties.[^4][^5]

**Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*** addresses perfectibility (human nature), perfecting (laws, institutions), and improvement (morals, laws).[^6]

**Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL III*** examines improvement and perfectibility in contexts of race, species capabilities, and societal progress.[^7]

**Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL IV*** explores improvement (physical, moral, industrial faculties) and perfecting under slavery and related systems.[^8]

**Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété: VOL I*** and **VOL II** touch on improvements (to land, inventions) and their valuation.[^9]

Several ancient Indian Smṛti texts mention "improvement" or "progress" in narrow legal/technical senses (e.g., land/house improvements, progressive weakening of heirs, punishments), but do not conceptually engage as philosophical notions of human/societal advancement.[^10] Poetic works like Allama Iqbal's *Bang-E-Dara* use "perfection" metaphorically, without systematic treatment.[^11] *Manusmṛti* employs "perfection" spiritually (e.g., soul's attainment), unrelated to modern perfectibility/progress.[^12]

No other works in the corpus meaningfully engage these concepts. The 37-work corpus lacks coverage of every topic.

[^1]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, [00-introduction/translation/#p-a32ab7](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/00-introduction/translation/#p-a32ab7).

[^2]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, [01-ce-que-lauteur-entend-par-le-mot-liberte/translation/#p-b8546d](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/01-ce-que-lauteur-entend-par-le-mot-liberte/translation/#p-b8546d).

[^3]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, [02-i-influence-de-la-race-sur-la-liberte/translation/#p-4b4f8f](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/02-i-influence-de-la-race-sur-la-liberte/translation/#p-4b4f8f).

[^4]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, [14-iv-des-conditions-auxquelles-toute-industrie-peut-/translation/#p-651f43](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/14-iv-des-conditions-auxquelles-toute-industrie-peut-/translation/#p-651f43).

[^5]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, [17-vii-de-la-liberte-de-lindustrie-agricole-130/translation/#p-9a36a0](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/17-vii-de-la-liberte-de-lindustrie-agricole-130/translation/#p-9a36a0).

[^6]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, [02-de-la-methode-analytique-appliquee-a-l-etude-de-la/translation/#p-5219cf](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/02-de-la-methode-analytique-appliquee-a-l-etude-de-la/translation/#p-5219cf).

[^7]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL III*, [47-parallele-entre-les-diverses-especes-d-hommes-de-l/translation/#p-696635](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/47-parallele-entre-les-diverses-especes-d-hommes-de-l/translation/#p-696635).

[^8]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL IV*, [03-de-l-influence-de-l-esclavage-sur-la-constitution-/translation/#p-cdad76](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iv-bca040/03-de-l-influence-de-l-esclavage-sur-la-constitution-/translation/#p-cdad76).

[^9]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété: VOL I*, [23-de-la-valeur-donnee-a-des-proprietes-particulieres/translation/#p-19bded](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/23-de-la-valeur-donnee-a-des-proprietes-particulieres/translation/#p-19bded).

[^10]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, [64/translation/#p-9829d2](/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/64/translation/#p-9829d2).

[^11]: Bang-E-Dara Part 3, [22-taleem-aur-iske-nataaij/translation/#p-da36f6](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/22-taleem-aur-iske-nataaij/translation/#p-da36f6).

[^12]: Manusmṛti, [02/translation/#p-fc9632](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-fc9632).
